free_text: Experimental determination of the 7-Be+p scattering
lengths
Final version
The paper has been submitted in
Nuclear Physics A. All Data were taken from the
Short communication
LEDA Detector
system.
GANIL,
The target consisted of a thin polyethylene
self-supporting foil with a very thin Au coating
evaporated on its upstream face.  The Au layer was used
for normalization.  The effect of this Au layer on the
beam energy loss was negligible.
Two PIPS (passivated implanted planar silicon)
detectors located at 60 degree.
The recoil protons were detected in two LEDA 4
multi-strip systems located at 12.6 and 61.7 cm from
the target.  A 12 mum thick Mylar foil was positioned
in front of all LEDA sectors to stop the high energy
scattered 7Be and the recoil 12C particles for the
beam energies of 6.2 and 5.6 MeV.
The experiment was carried out at He
RIB facility of the Centre de Recherches du Cyclotron.
Be-7 beam has been used.
Additional reference is added
Last checking has been done.
From a R-matrix analysis of the cross section data,   a
the energy and the width of the 1+ resonance (ex = 0.77
MeV) has been obtained.  The l = 0 scattering lengths
a/01 = 25+/-9 fm (channel spin I = 1) and a/02 = -7+/-3
Fm (channel spin I = 2) have been deduced.
Implications on the low energy S-factor of the
7Be(p,g)8B reaction are discussed.
The uncertainty is given in data table.
